debugLogging.title: Debug logging
debugLogging.description: Can be useful for troubleshooting issues with the mod.

modSettings.title: Mod settings
modSettings.description: Easily export or share your mod settings.

customList.descriptionInclusion: A custom list of additional executable file names/paths that the mod will target. The list is added to the inclusion list that the mod author defined to determine which processes to target. For each process, the mod is loaded if the executable file path matches one of the include entries and doesn't match any exclude entry.
customList.descriptionExclusion: A custom list of additional executable file names/paths that the mod will exclude from targeting. The list is added to the exclusion list that the mod author defined to determine which processes to target. For each process, the mod is loaded if the executable file path matches one of the include entries and doesn't match any exclude entry.
customList.processListPlaceholder: Process names/paths, one per line, for example:
customList.invalidCharactersWarning: The process list contains invalid characters which will be stripped: {{invalidCharacters}}

includeExcludeCustomOnly.title: Ignore mod inclusion/exclusion lists
includeExcludeCustomOnly.description: Ignore the mod's process inclusion/exclusion lists and use only the custom lists above.

patternsMatchCriticalSystemProcesses.title: Consider inclusion list patterns for critical system processes
patternsMatchCriticalSystemProcesses.description: By default, Windhawk only loads mods in critical system processes if the process is included without patterns, e.g. <0>critical.exe</0>, not <0>*</0> or <0>*.exe</0>. For more details about critical system processes, please refer to <1>the documentation</1>.

missions.workbenchDescription: Compare the strongest candidates, branch into follow-up queries, and open the top option without rebuilding the search from scratch.

settings.pageTitle: Tune Windhawk to match how you work
settings.pageDescription: Core behavior, interface preferences, and advanced controls are grouped here so you can shape the UI without digging through nested dialogs.

core.title: Core preferences
core.description: Daily-use behavior that affects discovery, language, and developer tools.

interface.title: Interface
interface.description: Local UI preferences that change only this webview experience.

layoutDensity.title: Layout density
layoutDensity.description: Choose whether the panel prioritizes breathing room or information density.

wideLayout.title: Use wide workspace
wideLayout.description: Expand the main content area to show more mods, settings, and code side by side.

reduceMotion.title: Reduce motion
reduceMotion.description: Minimize interface animations and transitions for a calmer, less distracting UI.

interface.resetDescription: Restore the local layout, width, and motion preferences to their defaults.

performance.title: Performance and AI
performance.description: Tune the workspace for responsiveness, efficiency, and future on-device AI workflows.
performance.recommendationFallback: Runtime diagnostics are not available yet, so the balanced profile remains the safest default.
performance.recommendationIssue: Windhawk detected a runtime configuration issue, so the efficient profile is recommended until the environment is stable again.
performance.recommendationNpu: An NPU was detected ({{npu}}), so the responsive profile and NPU-preferred AI acceleration are the strongest starting point.
performance.recommendationEfficient: {{memory}} GB of system memory suggests prioritizing the efficient profile to keep the UI lighter and calmer.
performance.recommendationResponsive: {{memory}} GB of system memory leaves room for a wider, faster-moving workspace, so the responsive profile is recommended.
performance.recommendationBalanced: This system looks stable without obvious constraints, so the balanced profile remains a sensible default.

profile.title: Workspace performance profile
profile.description: Choose whether this webview should favor balance, extra breathing room, or lower-overhead rendering.

aiAcceleration.title: On-device AI acceleration
aiAcceleration.description: Choose how strongly Windhawk should prefer NPU-backed local AI workflows when companion tools are available.

workflow.title: Workflow and discovery
workflow.description: Choose where Windhawk starts, how Explore behaves before search, and how much guidance the editor and Windows bridges should expose by default.

startupPage.title: Startup page
startupPage.description: Pick the first panel Windhawk opens when the webview starts without a deep link.

exploreDefaultSort.title: Explore default sort
exploreDefaultSort.description: Choose the starting sort order for Explore when no search query is active.

editorAssistance.title: Editor assistance level
editorAssistance.description: Control how much guidance the editor cockpit should surface while you are authoring a mod.

windowsQuickActions.title: Windows quick action density
windowsQuickActions.description: Choose whether About and the editor show only the most important Windows targets or the full action set.

authoring.title: Authoring and CLI
authoring.description: Set the default mod authoring format, visual studio preference, and command-line bridges used for Python rendering or Copilot-style helper flows.

language.title: Preferred authoring language
language.description: Choose whether New Mod Studio should default to classic C++ or Python-authored mods.

studioMode.title: Default studio mode
studioMode.description: Pick whether New Mod Studio should open in code-first or visual preset mode.

pythonCommand.title: Python renderer command
pythonCommand.description: Executable used when Windhawk renders a .wh.py mod into generated .wh.cpp output.

pythonArgs.title: Python renderer arguments
pythonArgs.description: Optional arguments passed before the renderer script, for example `-3` or `-X utf8`.

copilotCommand.title: Copilot bridge command
copilotCommand.description: Base executable used for CLI playbooks and Windhawk helper workflows.

copilotArgs.title: Copilot bridge arguments
copilotArgs.description: Default arguments appended after the Copilot bridge command.

language.title: Language
language.description: Select your preferred display language for Windhawk.

updates.title: Check for updates
updates.description: Automatically check for and notify about new versions of Windhawk and installed mods.

devMode.title: Developer mode
devMode.description: Show actions for developers, such as creating and modifying mods.

settings.advancedDescription: System-level options, troubleshooting controls, and deeper behavior tuning.

hideTrayIcon.title: Hide tray icon
hideTrayIcon.description: You will have to disable this option to exit Windhawk.

alwaysCompileModsLocally.title: Always compile mods locally
alwaysCompileModsLocally.description: By default, Windhawk downloads pre-compiled mod binaries from the Windhawk server when available. Enable this option to always compile mods locally instead.

parallelCompileTargets.title: Compile targets in parallel
parallelCompileTargets.description: Compile x86, x64, and ARM64 outputs concurrently when a mod targets more than one architecture.

preferPrecompiledHeaders.title: Prefer precompiled headers
preferPrecompiledHeaders.description: Reuse cached Windhawk headers when the workspace includes them to shorten repeated local builds.

requireElevation.title: Require UAC elevation for running Windhawk
requireElevation.description: Windhawk requires administrator rights, but for a single-user computer, getting the UAC prompt every time can be annoying, so Windhawk bypasses it. Enable this option to require UAC elevation for running Windhawk.

dontAutoShowToolkit.title: Don't automatically show the toolkit dialog
dontAutoShowToolkit.description: By default, Windhawk automatically shows the toolkit dialog when it detects that the taskbar isn't accessible, either due to system instability or for another reason. The toolkit dialog allows you to exit Windhawk, switch to safe mode, and perform other operations. The toolkit dialog can also be shown with the Ctrl+Win+W keyboard shortcut.

modInitDialogDelay.title: Mod initialization dialog delay
modInitDialogDelay.description: Number of milliseconds to wait before showing the progress dialog for mod initialization.

moreAdvancedSettings.title: More advanced settings
moreAdvancedSettings.restartNotice: Windhawk will be restarted to apply the settings.

loggingVerbosity.description: Logs can be viewed with a tool such as DebugView.

processList.descriptionExclusion: A list of process names/paths that Windhawk won't inject into. Can be useful for cases when Windhawk isn't compatible with a specific program. Note that adding a process to this list will not only prevent Windhawk from customizing it, but will also prevent Windhawk from intercepting child processes that this process creates. This will cause Windhawk to load mods with a slight delay in such cases.
processList.descriptionExclusionWiki: For more details about excluding processes and about the built-in process exclusion lists, please refer to <0>the documentation</0>.
processList.descriptionInclusion: A list of process names/paths that Windhawk will inject into, even if they're in the exclusion list.
processList.inclusionWithoutExclusionNotice: The process inclusion list has no effect with an empty process exclusion list.
processList.inclusionWithoutTotalExclusionNotice: If you meant to exclude all processes but these, you can set "*" in the process exclusion list.
processList.processListPlaceholder: Process names/paths, one per line, for example:
processList.invalidCharactersWarning: The process list contains invalid characters which will be stripped: {{invalidCharacters}}

about.title: Windhawk v{{version}}
about.subtitle: The customization marketplace for Windows and programs
about.pageDescription: Review the current workspace profile, confirm update and safety state, and jump to the key project resources from one place.

actions.openError: Unable to open the requested Windows target

status.title: Current status
status.description: A quick view of updates, safety, logging, and development state.

workspace.title: Workspace profile
workspace.description: The active settings that shape how Windhawk and this webview behave.

update.title: An update is available
update.subtitle: Consider updating Windhawk to get the latest features and bug fixes

modal.title: Updating Windhawk
modal.installingNote: Windhawk will restart automatically when the installation is complete.

links.title: Links
links.description: Project resources for releases, docs, source code, and translations.

runtime.title: Runtime diagnostics
runtime.description: Validate that the app, extension, and engine all target the same storage backend and review the resolved runtime paths used by this build.

issue.none: The engine and UI agree on the current storage layout.
issue.engineConfigMissing: The engine configuration file is missing, so installed mods can be listed in the UI but fail to load at runtime.
issue.engineStorageMismatch: The engine is targeting a different storage location than the Windhawk UI, which can prevent installed mods from loading.
issue.compilerMissing: The compiler toolchain is missing from the active Windhawk runtime, so mod creation and compilation fail until the Compiler directory is restored.
issue.fixHint: Use the repair action to rewrite the engine configuration so it matches the active Windhawk install.

actions.repairSuccess: Runtime configuration repaired. Windhawk will restart to apply it.
actions.repairFailed: Unable to repair the runtime configuration

windows.title: Windows environment
windows.description: Review the active Windows session and jump straight into the system surfaces that matter for Windhawk mods.
windows.quickActionsDescription: Open the Windows settings pages and runtime folders that are most useful when debugging or testing mods.

windowsUpdate.title: Windows Update
windowsUpdate.description: Jump to update controls before testing changes against a newer system build.

taskbar.title: Taskbar settings
taskbar.description: Open taskbar personalization to compare native Windows behavior with taskbar mods.

start.title: Start settings
start.description: Inspect Start menu layout and recommendations before testing Start-focused mods.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Open the native notification surface to baseline banners, badges, and the notification center.

multitasking.title: Multitasking
multitasking.description: Check Snap and Alt+Tab defaults when validating window-management changes.

colors.title: Colors
colors.description: Compare theme, transparency, and accent behavior against visual shell mods.

background.title: Background
background.description: Open wallpaper and desktop background settings before testing visual shell tweaks.

themes.title: Themes
themes.description: Inspect theme packs and visual defaults when comparing appearance-related mods.

lockScreen.title: Lock screen
lockScreen.description: Review lock screen and spotlight settings for login and background customization workflows.

clipboard.title: Clipboard
clipboard.description: Open clipboard history and sync settings for productivity and input-related mods.

startupApps.title: Startup apps
startupApps.description: Inspect startup behavior when testing tray, shell, and session-start integrations.

sound.title: Sound settings
sound.description: Open Windows audio controls for mods that touch volume, media, or device behavior.

appData.title: Open app data
appData.description: Jump to Windhawk's active data root in Explorer.

engine.title: Open engine folder
engine.description: Inspect the engine binaries and active runtime files in Explorer.

builtWith.title: Built with
builtWith.description: Core tools and libraries that shape the Windhawk experience.
builtWith.vscodium: A community-driven distribution of Microsoft's VSCode editor
builtWith.minHook: The minimalistic API hooking library for Windows
builtWith.others: Other tools and libraries, and a bit of code

changelogViewer.noMatches: No changelog entries match the current filter.

installModal.title: Install {{mod}}
installModal.warningDescription: Malicious mods can damage your computer or violate your privacy. Install mods only from authors whom you trust.
installModal.snapshotDescription: Review scope, freshness, and community context before you install the mod.
installModal.strategyDescription: Choose a safer or faster install path based on what you know about the mod right now.
installModal.reviewDescription: Open the details, source, or changelog tabs to inspect the mod without leaving this screen behind.
installModal.checklistDescription: A short verification checklist based on the mod's scope and reviewability.
installModal.verifiedTooltip: We verified that this profile belongs to the mod author, but note that <0>verified</0> is not the same as <0>trusted</0>. Make sure you trust the mod author, or carefully inspect the source code before installing.

newModStudio.title: New Mod Studio
newModStudio.copyError: Unable to copy the requested content

mode.title: Choose how you want to start
mode.description: Pick a code-first workflow or a visual preset flow, then choose whether the starter should land in classic C++ or Python authoring mode.
mode.codeDescription: Choose raw templates and hook-oriented scaffolds first.
mode.visualDescription: Start from outcomes such as automation, shell work, or settings design.

recent.title: Recent sessions
recent.description: Resume a recent starter, preset, or workflow with the same launch brief, packet, and editor-side context.
recent.empty: Launch a starter, preset, or workflow once and it will show up here for one-click relaunch.

authoring.title: Authoring target
authoring.description: Choose which source format and authoring workflow New Mod Studio should create by default.
authoring.cppDescription: Use the classic Windhawk source path and focused native templates.
authoring.pythonDescription: Author in Python and render back to compatible .wh.cpp output for compile-time use.

starters.title: Choose a starting point
starters.description: Start with a structured core template, a classic sample, or choose a focused starter for shell tweaks, Chromium browser work, window behavior, or settings-first mod design.
starters.pythonNote: Python mode currently ships with the automation starter. The other starters are still C++ templates.

visual.title: Visual presets
visual.description: Use a higher-level preset to start from outcomes such as automation, Explorer shell work, window behavior, or settings design instead of picking a raw source template.

workflows.title: Workflow bundles
workflows.description: Use guided launch bundles when you want a starter, checklist, CLI tools, and AI prompts that fit the kind of mod you are building.

prompts.title: AI prompt packs
prompts.description: Copy these prompts into your AI assistant to ideate, structure, scaffold, review, or document a mod while keeping the Windhawk workflow intact.

cli.title: CLI playbooks
cli.description: Quick-copy commands that line up with the Windhawk Copilot helper workflow for runtime detection, source setup, compile, and restart.

newModStudio.footerNote: AI can accelerate scaffolding and review, but you should still validate hook targets, safety, compatibility, and manual test results yourself before shipping a mod.

setupAssistant.title: Choose your first-launch workspace
setupAssistant.description: Pick a starter profile now. You can revisit these settings at any time from the Settings page.

devModeAction.message: Creating and modifying mods requires some knowledge of C/C++ development for Windows. AI can help with scaffolding and review, but you still need to understand and verify the generated code before using it.

safeMode.alert: Windhawk is running in safe mode, all code injection functionality is turned off.
safeMode.offConfirm: Windhawk will be restarted to apply the settings.

modPreview.actionUnavailable: Action is not available in preview mode
modPreview.notCompiled: Mod needs to be compiled before it can be previewed

sidebar.editorDescription: Compile, inspect, and iterate on the current mod without leaving the editor workflow.
sidebar.copyError: Unable to copy the requested text
sidebar.openError: Unable to open the requested Windows surface

sections.launchDescription: Keep the starter, workflow, and tooling context visible after the editor opens so the launcher does not collapse into a blank draft.
sections.statusDescription: A quick read on the current draft, build state, and targeting scope.
sections.evidenceDescription: Research-driven heuristics for safer runs, clearer scope, and stronger AI prompts.
sections.windowsDescription: Jump straight into the Windows surfaces that match this mod's inferred shell scope.
sections.provocationsDescription: Research-backed counterquestions that push the draft beyond agreement and toward better evidence.
sections.verificationDescription: Turn the current draft into a practical checklist and a release-ready evidence packet.
sections.aiDescription: Copy contextual prompts that use the current mod id and metadata as a starting point.
sections.workflowDescription: A short next-step plan that reacts to the current draft, build state, and process scope.

descriptions.enableMod: Controls whether the compiled mod should load after a successful build.
descriptions.enableLogging: Keep logging enabled while iterating so new failures are easier to diagnose.

compileModes.currentDescription: Keep the next build aligned with the current runtime profile: {{mode}}.
compileModes.disabledDescription: Build the binary, but keep the mod unloaded while you inspect the result.
compileModes.loggingDescription: Make the first live run more observable so regressions are easier to localize.
compileModes.disabledLoggingDescription: Use the safest, highest-evidence first run for broad or unstable drafts.

ai.understandingDescription: Mirrors recent code-understanding research by turning common explainer requests into one-click, context-rich prompts.
ai.challengeDescription: Push the assistant to question the current design instead of merely agreeing with it.
ai.validationDescription: Use feedback-loop prompts when you need stronger tests, safer docs, or a smaller recovery path after build problems.
ai.buildDescription: Keep the original scaffolding and context-pack flows for broader rewrites or handoffs.

workflow.shortcutBody: The compile action is wired into the editor workflow, so you can rebuild without hunting for the sidebar button.
workflow.safeCompileTitle: Use disabled builds for risky changes
workflow.safeCompileBody: Compile disabled first when you are changing hook targets, process scope, or startup behavior.
workflow.loggingTitle: Turn on logging for first runs
workflow.loggingBody: Logging is most useful right after a refactor, compile error, or API change because it makes regressions easier to localize.

sidebar.footerNote: Scroll through every editor control while the exit action stays pinned here.
sidebar.exitConfirmation: Changes since the last successful compilation will be lost
